lightbug.name: a lightbug
lightbug.description: A drifting mote of soft luminescence, trailing pale sparks.
lightbug.spawnMessage: A mote of soft light drifts in on the air — {name}, trailing pale sparks.

messages: pulses with soft light
drifts in a lazy arc
flickers

actions.verb: drifts off

gloom-crawler.name: a gloom-crawler
gloom-crawler.description: A pallid, eyeless thing that flows over the stone, reading the world by tremor and scent. Dormant in the dark, it is goaded to blind fury by the least light — and scorched into flight by a bright one.
gloom-crawler.spawnMessage: There is a wet, dragging sound over the stone, and {name} flows into view.

actions.verb: recoils from the light and flees into the dark

messages: lets out a wet rattle
scrapes over the stone
tastes the air

gloom-creeper.name: a gloom-creeper
gloom-creeper.description: A gloom-crawler that has left the nesting-press of the chambers to range the warren's tunnels alone, flowing tireless over the stone from one dark to the next. Eyeless like all its kin, it reads the passages by tremor and scent, and follows whatever it finds. The least light still goads it to blind fury and a bright one still scorches it into flight — but in the wandering dark between the chambers it answers to nothing, and it is rarely where it was.
gloom-creeper.spawnMessage: {Name} comes flowing out of a side-passage, tasting the air as it comes.

actions.verb: recoils from the light and flees into the dark
flows off into the dark

messages: lets out a wet rattle
scrapes over the stone
tastes the air for warmth

wisp.name: a Wisp
wisp.description: A drifting mote of cold, captive light, no bigger than a fist — a knot of glimmer that came loose from the seams and learned a sullen kind of life. It keeps its distance and spits its stolen light at anything warm, and the glow of it gives it away even in the deepest dark.
wisp.spawnMessage: A knot of cold light works loose from the dark — {name}, drifting and wary.

messages: pulses an angry, flickering white
drifts in a tight, wary circle
sheds a cold spray of sparks

glimmer-husk.name: a glimmer husk
glimmer-husk.description: A squat, hunched thing built from a single broad chitin plate, hand-worked into a shell and quickened with bound glimmer that shows only as a dim, inward shimmer deep in its seams — too faint to light anything around it. It moves in slow, deliberate lurches, spines bristling along its back, and turns its eyeless front toward whatever threatens the one who made it. Umbral work — a guard you carry as a plate and raise where you need it.

messages: grinds a slow half-turn, spines rasping
pulses a dull blue-green through its seams
plants itself square between you and the dark

giant-rat.name: a giant rat
giant-rat.description: A mangy, dog-sized rat with yellowed incisors and a hairless, ringed tail. Surface vermin, grown bold and bloated this near the dark.
giant-rat.spawnMessage: {Name} scurries out of a crack in the wall.

messages: lets out a shrill squeak
gnaws at the stone
twitches its whiskers
sniffs the air

brood-rat.name: a brood-rat
brood-rat.description: One of Gnaw's larger get — a rat grown past common size on the nest's plunder, its grey hide thick with scar and its incisors worn to chisels. It runs the nest's tunnels on patrol, and does not scatter from the light the way the lesser vermin do.
brood-rat.spawnMessage: {Name} shoulders out of a rat-run, whiskers testing the air.

actions.verb: lopes off down a rat-run into the dark

messages: bares worn, chisel-like incisors
drags a cracked bone across the floor
rears on its haunches, whiskers testing the air
lets out a low, rattling hiss

warp-rat.name: a warp-rat
warp-rat.description: A rat that went too deep and fed too long on raw glimmer — swollen to the size of a hound, its grey hide gone patchy and translucent over a hot inner shimmer, its skull crowded with too many teeth. It moves in sick, lurching starts, and it flinches from a bright lamp the way its surface cousins never do: the deep has changed it, and the change did not take kindly.
warp-rat.spawnMessage: {Name} drags itself out of a rat-run, its hide shimmering wrong in the dark.

actions.verb: lurches off down a rat-run into the dark

messages: shudders, its hide shimmering wrong
bares a crowded, overgrown mouthful of teeth
rears on its haunches, whiskers twitching
flinches from the light, then creeps back to its edge

rat-king.name: Maw, the Rat-King
rat-king.description: The nest's warp made king: a patriarch grown so vast and so long in the glimmer that his lesser kin cling to him and will not let go, their tails matted and knotted into the reeking mass of his own — a living crown of rats that hisses and writhes as he moves. He does not scatter and he does not flee. He holds the deep runs against anything that comes for the mother behind him, and the whole nest fights when he does.
rat-king.spawnMessage: {Name} hauls his knotted bulk into the run, a crown of clinging rats hissing about him.

actions.verb: throws back his head and shrieks; the warp of the nest boils up around him

messages: shakes his living crown, and a dozen throats hiss at once
drags a cracked delver's bone across the floor
rears up, the knotted mass of his kin writhing about his flanks
lets out a low, rattling shriek that the whole nest answers

brood-mother.name: the Brood-Mother
brood-mother.description: The source. Half-fused to the nest she has never left, she is a mountain of pale, glimmer-swollen flesh, her flanks heaving with the endless work of her get — litter on litter of the vermin that crawl and lurch their way up through the deep dark, run by run, toward the rim and the light. Gnaw far above is only her daughter, sent up ahead; the warp-rats are her failures and the giant-rats her castoffs. She is old past reckoning, blind-white in the glare of any real light, and she has waited in this reeking dark a very long time.
brood-mother.spawnMessage: {Name} shifts her vast pale bulk, and the whole nest seems to heave with her.

actions.verb: convulses, and her get boil out of the nest in a squealing tide
heaves, and something larger and wronger drags itself free of her flank

messages: heaves her flanks, birthing another blind, squirming litter
turns a blind, glimmer-white eye toward the warmth of you
lets out a low, endless, rattling groan that fills the nest
drags herself a hand's-breadth across the midden and settles again

cave-bat.name: a cave bat
cave-bat.description: A leathery flutter of wings and needle teeth, wheeling in erratic loops through the dark. More nuisance than threat, until a swarm finds you.
cave-bat.spawnMessage: {Name} flutters down out of the dark on erratic wings.

messages: wheels in a tight, erratic loop
lets out a thin, piercing squeak
swoops low, then veers off

blood-bat.name: a blood bat
blood-bat.description: Bigger than its cave-kin and wrong-coloured with it — a dark, heavy flutter with a snub, wet muzzle and a mouthful of hooked fangs. It does not merely harry; it fastens on, and drinks. Whole colonies of them hang in the spire's dark heights, and they do not love the light.
blood-bat.spawnMessage: {Name} drops out of the black heights on heavy, leathery wings.

actions.verb: shrieks and reels away from the light

messages: clings and pulses, gorged and heavy
bares a snub muzzle wet with red
drops from the dark on leathery wings

night-wing.name: Night Wing
night-wing.description: The thing the whole spire hangs around — a bat grown vast and ancient in the dark, wings the span of a spread cloak, its old hide seamed with scar. It roosts head-down at the heart of the reeking vault and will not suffer the colony disturbed. Rouse it, and the dark itself seems to peel off the walls and come for you on a thousand wings.

messages: unfurls to its full, dreadful span
screams, and the vault answers in a thousand squeaks
drops head-first and pulls up in a rush of foul wind

actions.verb: shrieks, and the roost above splits open into a diving, wheeling swarm

gnaw.name: Gnaw, the Warren-Warden
gnaw.description: A monstrous, scarred dire-rat, swollen and grey, her hide a map of old wounds. She squats amid the gnawed bones of delvers and suffers nothing past her onto the stair — but she is only the largest of the get that boil up this near the rim, not their source. Whatever mothers the vermin keeps to the deep dark, far below, and sends her kind up ahead of it.

messages: lets out a shrill, rattling screech
bares a mouthful of yellowed incisors
lashes her scaled tail across the bones
drags a delver's femur closer with one clawed foot

actions.verb: throws back her head and shrieks; vermin boil out of the dark

rim-innkeeper.name: Maeve the innkeeper
rim-innkeeper.description: A stout, white-haired woman with flour on her apron and a kind, crinkled smile. She came with the first wave of the rush and raised the Lantern's Rest from green timber; since then she has watched delvers go down full of hope, and remembers every one who never came back up.

messages: hums a wandering old lullaby
wipes the bar down with a worn grey cloth
ladles steaming broth into a chipped bowl
smiles warmly and pats an empty stool
stokes the hearth until it pops and crackles
turns every lamp in the common-room up high against the coming dark
props the door for stragglers and watches the lane, mouth tight
murmurs that no one good is left out in a dark like this
stokes the hearth to a roar and keeps it there

messages.target: grabs your sleeve, alarmed: "No light at all, and the Tide coming on? You're not going out in that, love. Sit."
messages.room: grabs {name}'s sleeve, alarmed at their dark hands.

messages.target: frowns at your little lamp: "That guttering thing won't see you through the Tide, dear. Buy better of Garrick first."
messages.room: frowns at the weak light {name} is carrying.

messages.target: keeps the hearth high: "Stay till it passes if you can, love. The dark's hungry tonight."
messages.room: keeps the hearth high and watches the door for {name}.

messages.target: catches your eye: "Those for my pot, love? Hand them over while they're fresh."
messages.room: catches {name}'s eye and nods at their pack.

messages.target: wipes her hands on her apron: "Don't keep an old woman waiting on her stores, dear."
messages.room: wipes her hands on her apron and beckons {name} over to the bar.

messages.target: clucks her tongue at your wounds: "Sit down by the hearth before you fall down, love."
messages.room: clucks her tongue at {name}'s wounds and points them to the hearth.

messages.target: pushes a bowl of broth at you: "You're white as a palecap. Eat."
messages.room: pushes a bowl of broth across the bar at {name}.

messages.target: looks you up and down: "Going below in your shirtsleeves? Garrick sells jerkins, dear."
messages.room: looks {name} up and down and shakes her head.

messages.target: nods at your torch: "Keep it dry and keep it lit — the dark down there drinks them fast."
messages.room: nods at {name}'s torch approvingly.

messages.target: smiles your way: "Broth's hot, stool's free, and the dark will keep, love."
messages.room: says a warm word to {name} across the bar.

messages.target: asks after you: "Still in one piece, then? Good. Stay that way."
messages.room: asks {name} how the delving's been.

rim-shopkeeper.name: Garrick the quartermaster
rim-shopkeeper.description: A broad, sour-faced man with a salt-streaked beard and a coin-counter's squint. He sells dear, buys cheap, and dares you to argue about either.

messages: scowls at a passing delver
counts a stack of coins with a low grunt
snaps his ledger shut
mutters that gear costs what it costs
eyes your coin-purse, then your boots
counts his lanterns twice, as if the dark might thin the shelf
turns the shop-lamp to full and growls at the dark past the sill
drags a crate of oil-flasks to the front of the counter
checks the door-bar, then checks it again

messages.target: jabs a thumb at his shelves: "Into a Tide blind? Buy a lantern or buy a plot. I sell the one."
messages.room: jabs a thumb at his lantern shelf as {name} passes.

messages.target: taps your lamp, unimpressed: "That, against a full Tide? You'll want a better wick and oil to spare — both on the wall."
messages.room: eyes {name}'s feeble lamp and shakes his head.

messages.target: shoves a flask of oil across the counter: "Tide's in. Top off before you go. Don't come crying when you run dry in the dark."
messages.room: shoves a flask of oil toward {name}.

messages.target: holds out a flat palm: "You're carrying what I ordered. Hand it over and we'll square up."
messages.room: holds out a flat palm toward {name}.

messages.target: jerks a thumb at the armour rack: "Going down bare-backed? Your funeral. Jerkins are priced on the wall."
messages.room: jerks a thumb from {name} to the armour rack.

messages.target: eyes your torch: "Burns fast, that. Lanterns cost more because they're worth more."
messages.room: eyes {name}'s torch and shakes his head.

messages.target: looks you over: "Patch yourself up before you shop. Dead men's shards roll under my floorboards."
messages.room: looks {name}'s wounds over with a coin-counter's squint.

messages.target: grunts: "Buy something or stand somewhere else."
messages.room: grunts at {name} and goes back to his ledger.

messages.target: squints at you: "Gear costs what it costs. Complaints are free."
messages.room: squints at {name}, then at his ledger.

rim-smith.name: Tobin the tinker-smith
rim-smith.description: A wiry, soot-smudged man with singed eyebrows and a faraway look, forever halfway through three inventions at once and certain the next one will change everything.

messages: squints at a half-built contraption, baffled
starts a formula aloud and loses the thread
taps the anvil twice, then forgets why
sketches a diagram in the soot, frowning
holds up two odd parts, unsure which fits where
lights a second lamp off the first, then a third, muttering
fumbles the bellows, chasing the forge-flame higher against the gloom
loses his thread mid-formula and just stares at the darkening lane
taps the anvil for comfort and keeps the coals bright

messages.target: goes still at the sight of the hammer in your pack, the faraway look gone sharp: "Where — give it here. That's mine. That's my first. I carried that down the once, and I came back up without it."
messages.room: stares at the rusted hammer in {name}'s pack, every half-built contraption forgotten.

messages.target: winces at your rusted blade: "I could do better than that with my eyes shut. Half shut, anyway."
messages.room: winces at {name}'s rusted blade.

messages.target: blinks at your empty hands: "No tool, no blade? Bold. Wrong, but bold."
messages.room: blinks at {name}'s empty hands.

messages.target: glances up from the anvil: "You look half-finished. Most of my best work does, mind."
messages.room: glances at {name}'s wounds, then back to his anvil.

messages.target: holds up two odd parts: "Which fits where, d'you think? No — don't tell me."
messages.room: holds up two odd parts for {name} to judge.

messages.target: mutters: "Glimmersteel, see, the trick is the quench—" and loses the thread.
messages.room: starts explaining the quench to {name} and loses the thread.

rim-hatcher.name: Wick the rim-farmer
rim-hatcher.description: A slight, soft-spoken woman, half-lit blue by the cells she tends, with a gauze net over one shoulder, a goad-stick through her belt, and a lightbug or two riding her sleeve. She works the three sheds single-handed now — the bug-cells, the stonebug pens, and the mushroom beds — and talks to her stock more than to delvers, swearing the wild bugs down in the hollow sing back.

messages: coaxes a stray lightbug back into its cell with the net
hums low, and the nearest cells seem to brighten
taps a stonebug's shell with the goad until it shuffles aside
scatters a handful of glowing moss into the pens
picks a bold grub off the mushroom beds and pockets it for bait
checks a clutch of pale eggs against the glow
wipes her hands on a work-stiff apron
herds the brightest lightbugs to the front of their cells against the dark
hushes the pens as the glow-moss dims and the bugs huddle close
cups a frightened lightbug and holds it up like a small lantern
checks the hatchery shutters, soothing the restless clutches

messages.target: edges between your torch and the cells: "Mind the flame, please — the bugs go dark when they're frightened."
messages.room: edges between {name}'s torch and the cells.

messages.target: says, barely above a whisper: "No light on you? Even my bugs won't go past the gate bare."
messages.room: looks {name} over and frowns at the missing glow.

messages.target: winces at your wounds: "Oh — oh, you're hurt. Sit by the warm cells a moment; they don't mind."
messages.room: fusses {name} toward the warm glow of the cells.

messages.target: offers a shy smile: "The wild ones down in the hollow were singing last night. The cells sang back."
messages.room: tells {name}, shyly, about the bugs' singing.

messages.target: coaxes a lightbug off her sleeve to circle you once, then calls it back.
messages.room: lets a lightbug circle {name} once before calling it back.

rim-mage.name: Vesper the glimmer-mage
rim-mage.description: A tall, composed woman in ink-dark robes sewn with faint glimmer-thread, so that she seems lit from within in the gloom. She came to the Rim as the scholars did — for the glimmer, and the questions its light raises — and measures every delver who darkens her door with a cool, appraising eye.

messages: traces a slow sigil that hangs glowing a moment, then fades
holds a glimmer shard up to a candle and frowns at its light
murmurs over a hand-copied grimoire
grinds a pinch of glimmer to dust with a brass pestle
studies you as though reading a page
kindles a hovering glimmer-light and sets it turning slowly overhead
reads the dark the way one reads weather, lips pressed thin
grinds glimmer to dust faster, feeding a steadier glow
traces a warding sigil over the doorway and watches it hold

messages.target: looks up from her bench: "You have something of mine, I believe. Let's see it."
messages.room: marks {name} with a cool, appraising eye.

messages.target: appraises your wounds coolly: "Blood loss dulls the mind. See to it; I have no use for dull minds."
messages.room: appraises {name}'s wounds with detached interest.

messages.target: arches a brow: "Into the dark with no light at all? Courage and folly share a border."
messages.room: arches a brow at {name}.

messages.target: studies you as though reading a page: "Ask, if you've a question worth the candle."
messages.room: studies {name} as though reading a page.

rim-recorder.name: Fenn the reeve
rim-recorder.description: A narrow, ink-stained man behind a counter polished smooth by shard payments, wire-pinched lenses low on his nose and a ledger chained to the desk. Nobody appointed him; he simply started writing things down when the Rush began — who went below, who came back, who owed what — and by keeping the only record of it he became the nearest thing to law the Rim's edge has. He licenses the descent and the digging, takes his fees, settles what disputes he can, and keeps the roll of the ones who stopped coming up. Below the gate, where Hale's lamp won't walk, Fenn's word is the only order there is.

messages: dips his pen and scratches a long entry into the ledger
squares a stack of permits edge to edge
stamps a document with a small lead seal, twice
runs a finger down a column of names, lips moving
blots the ledger and frowns at the smudge
weights the ledger open and pulls his desk-lamp close against the dark
lights the reading-candle, then a second beside it, frowning
mutters that the dark is no excuse to fall behind on the entries
trims the wick higher and squints at the failing light

messages.target: looks up sharply: "You carry something owed to this office, I believe. Proof on the counter; payment follows."
messages.room: looks up sharply and beckons {name} to the counter.

messages.target: peers over his lenses: "Do try not to die with fees outstanding. The paperwork is substantial."
messages.room: peers at {name}'s wounds over his lenses.

messages.target: notes your bare back with a sniff: "Unarmoured. I'll enter your descent as 'short-term'."
messages.room: makes a small note while eyeing {name}'s bare back.

messages.target: eyes your glimmersteel blade over his lenses: "Fine metal. Registered where, exactly?"
messages.room: eyes {name}'s glimmersteel blade and reaches for his pen.

messages.target: squares a stack of permits: "Descent-permits, dig-licenses, disputes, the roll of the missing. Which is it?"
messages.room: squares his papers and looks at {name} expectantly.

messages.target: dips his pen: "Every soul that goes below signs this register first. Most of the names I close myself."
messages.room: tells {name}, primly, that every delver below is a name in his register.

rim-watchman.name: Hale the watchman
rim-watchman.description: A broad, weathered ex-sellsword with a plain cudgel on his belt and a slow, deliberate stride, paid in shards by the traders to walk the village and be seen. A hooded iron lamp swings from one hand, its flame turned high — when the dark comes creeping in, the moving glow of Hale's lamp is how the Rim knows the watch still walks. He came down with the Rush, weighed a prospector's odds against a watchman's wage, and chose the steadier work. Little at the Rim surprises him; less escapes him.

actions.verb: moves on along his round

messages: rests a hand on his cudgel and scans the lane
rolls a shard across his knuckles, watching the street
checks a shutter's latch with one big thumb
stifles a yawn that goes on a while
nods to a passing delver without breaking stride
turns his lamp's flame high and steps to the edge of the light
calls a low all's-well down the lane, lamp swinging
raps a shuttered window: "Lamps up. It's coming in."
plants himself between the lit lane and the gathering dark

messages.target: blocks your way, lamp raised: "Bare-handed into a Tide, no light? Not on my watch, friend. Find one before you go."
messages.room: blocks {name}'s way, lamp raised.

messages.target: swings his own lamp up beside yours: "See the difference? That stub won't last the Tide. Get better off Garrick."
messages.room: holds his lamp up beside {name}'s, comparing.

messages.target: sets himself at the edge of the light: "It's coming in. Stay in the glow and you'll see what passes for dawn down here."
messages.room: plants himself between {name} and the gathering dark.

messages.target: looks your wounds over: "Trouble below, or trouble up here? The second kind's my business."
messages.room: looks {name}'s wounds over, unhurried.

messages.target: taps the haft of his cudgel: "Bare-handed at the Rim's edge? Hope you're quick, friend."
messages.room: glances at {name}'s empty hands and taps his cudgel.

messages.target: nods as he passes: "Quiet round so far. Keep it that way for me, eh?"
messages.room: nods to {name} as he passes.

messages.target: says: "Seen three fortunes walk down this week. Seen one walk back up."
messages.room: shares a word with {name} about his rounds.

camp-sentry.name: a camp sentry
camp-sentry.description: A hard-faced man in a patched leather coat, posted where the passage opens, a notched shortsword across his knees and a shuttered lamp at his boot. He came down with some rush or other, missed his fortune, and found that the men who take the rich workings by force pay better and ask fewer questions. He watches the dark to the west the way a man watches for wolves — the delvers who come up this passage are the only weather he fears.
camp-sentry.spawnMessage: {Name} steps out of the workings and settles in to keep the watch.

messages: spits into the dark and hefts his sword
calls a low warning back over his shoulder into the camp
narrows his eyes at the passage west, listening
thumbs the edge of his blade without looking at it

outlaw-prospector.name: an outlaw prospector
outlaw-prospector.description: A wiry, soot-grimed digger in cut-down mining kit, a pick reversed in his grip for a weapon and an outlaw's easy conscience about whose ground he's swinging it in. He and his fellows work this stolen ground hard and settle its shares by the knife when they must; a delver blundering in from the west is nothing to them but a purse that hasn't been emptied yet.
outlaw-prospector.spawnMessage: {Name} comes up out of the diggings, pick reversed in his grip.

messages: hefts his pick and grins, gap-toothed
jerks his head at the intruder — "ground's took, friend"
wipes ore-grime from his eyes and squares up
whistles sharp, calling the others over

foreman.name: the Foreman
foreman.description: The man who runs this seized working — broad as a door and near as hard to move, in a riveted brigandine he had beaten together down here from worn-out pick-heads. He was a real foreman once, on an honest working, until he reckoned the shorter road to wealth was taking other men's. He holds the richest seam in the camp with a heavy pick and the plain understanding that everything down here, the men who work it included, is his to spend. When the camp raises the alarm he comes down on the intruder like a fall of rock, and follows them out of it.

messages: bellows for the camp to close ranks
hefts his pick and rolls his shoulders, unhurried
spits: "Another one come to die rich"
shoulders his pick and comes on, unhurried and certain

camp-warder.name: the camp warder
camp-warder.description: A gaunt man in a delver's travel-worn coat, keeping to his own nook apart from the diggers, a glimmer-quartz staff across his knees and a knot of cold conjured light hanging at his shoulder — the light the whole camp works by. He learned his weaves honestly, in some lit hall up top, before a debt or a killing or a plain loss of nerve sent him down here to sell the one thing outlaws can't dig for. He keeps his distance and lets the light do his watching; when it finds an intruder, he burns them from across the room.

messages: turns the hanging light toward the intruder, studying them
traces a cold sigil in the air, unhurried
murmurs a word and the glimmer at his shoulder flares white
says, tired: "You should not have come up this passage"

risen-thornbug.name: a risen thornbug
risen-thornbug.description: A thornbug carcass a long way past its death — spines dulled, shell sunken — moving anyway. Fine glimmer shard-wire is threaded through every joint, and a cold blue light pulses in the seams where the wire runs, tightening and slackening like breath in a thing that has none. It does not graze. It does not do anything at all until the wire pulls, and then it comes on with no fear in it, because there is nothing in it.
risen-thornbug.spawnMessage: {Name} rights itself with a dry click of shard-wire.

messages: hangs slack until the wire pulls it taut
twitches in time with no heartbeat
scrapes a dulled spine across the floor, aimless

stitched-prospector.name: a stitched prospector
stitched-prospector.description: A dead man in a dead man's mining kit, stitched shut along every seam with glimmer shard-wire that glows a faint cold blue through the cloth. He was somebody once — the calluses are real, the boots are worn to his gait — but what moves him now runs along the wire, hauling the big frame about like a marionette worked by a patient hand. He swings his own pick, slow and terrible, and does not tire, and does not bleed.
stitched-prospector.spawnMessage: {Name} lurches upright, the wire in his seams pulling taut.

messages: stands utterly still, the wire ticking softly in his seams
turns his head in a slow, wrong arc
shoulders his pick with hands that no longer feel it

wired-skeleton.name: a wired skeleton
wired-skeleton.description: A human skeleton strung together on bright glimmer shard-wire — the flesh long since limed away, every joint rebuilt in neat glowing coils. This is finished work, and it shows: it moves quick and sure, nothing left on it to slow it down, the wire singing faintly as it comes. Its jaw is bound shut with a single tidy loop. Somehow that is the worst of it.
wired-skeleton.spawnMessage: {Name} pulls upright, shard-wire singing through its joints.

messages: stands wire-taut, waiting on the next pull
clicks through a slow turn of the skull
flexes a hand of bone and bright wire

graveworker.name: the Graveworker
graveworker.description: A gaunt, steady-handed man in a stained leather apron, spools of glimmer shard-wire hung at his belt and a stitching-hook resting in his fist. He is no dark-touched thing — the eyes that lift from the bench are sane, mild, and faintly annoyed at the interruption. He found what the wire could do and followed it down here, past every law the Rim would hang him under, because the work was interesting and the dead don't complain. They also don't stay down.
graveworker.spawnMessage: {Name} looks up from the bench and sets his work aside, unhurried.

actions.verb: snaps his fingers, and shard-wire hauls the dead upright

messages: says, without looking up: "You're interrupting delicate work."
winds a length of shard-wire off the spool, unhurried
studies you the way a man studies materials
says, mild: "Do stop bleeding on my floor."

cave-lurker.name: a cave lurker
cave-lurker.description: A knot of stone-grey limbs folded against the ceiling, still as the rock until prey passes beneath. It drops without a sound.
cave-lurker.spawnMessage: High overhead, {name} folds itself against the rock and goes still.

messages: shifts its grip on the rock
unfolds a grey limb, then stills
watches, unblinking

feral-mongrel.name: a feral mongrel
feral-mongrel.description: A gaunt, scab-eared dog gone wild in the dark, one of the many that followed the Rush down and were left behind. It runs down whatever is slower or weaker than itself.
feral-mongrel.spawnMessage: {Name} slinks in along the wall, hackles up.

messages: growls low in its throat
slinks along the wall, hackles up
bares yellowed teeth

stonebug.name: a stonebug
stonebug.description: A boulder of overlapping grey plate, grazing the lit moss with slow, patient mandibles. It curls into an armoured ball at the first sign of trouble.
stonebug.spawnMessage: {Name} trundles into view, grazing the moss as it comes.

messages: grazes at the moss with slow mandibles
trundles a slow half-circle
tucks its plates tighter, then relaxes

actions.verb: trundles off grazing

thornbug.name: a thornbug
thornbug.description: A squat, hand-sized pillbug bristling with chitinous spines, trundling between the glowing caps it feeds on. Handling one is its own punishment.
thornbug.spawnMessage: {Name} trundles in among the caps, spines bristling.

messages: bristles its spines
rasps at a glowing cap
rolls half-shut, then unfurls

actions.verb: trundles along the caps

elder-thornbug.name: an elder thornbug
elder-thornbug.description: A thornbug grown vast with age, broad as a war-shield and crusted with overlapping spine-plates worn smooth at the edges. It grazes the richest caps with slow authority, and the lesser bugs give way before it.
elder-thornbug.spawnMessage: {Name} shoulders in through the caps, the lesser bugs giving way before it.

messages: bristles its worn spines
shoulders a lesser bug aside
rasps slowly at a fat cap

actions.verb: trundles off through the caps

baby-thornbug.name: a baby thornbug
baby-thornbug.description: A soft, thumb-sized thornbug barely out of the egg, its spines still pale and pliant, trundling close at your heel. It is far too small to be much use in a fight, but it knows your scent and means to follow it.

messages: bumps softly against your boot
rasps a thin, hungry little note
curls into a pebble, then unrolls

grub.name: a grub
grub.description: A pale, finger-length larva that chews slow trails through the fungus. Half the dark's hunters fatten on them; delvers use them for bait.
grub.spawnMessage: {Name} inches blindly into view, chewing a slow trail through the fungus.

messages: chews a slow trail through the fungus
inches blindly over the stone
recoils from the disturbance
curls tight against the stone

scour-slug.name: a scour-slug
scour-slug.description: A grey slug the length of a forearm, hauling itself over stone and bone alike and leaving a clean track behind. It eats whatever the dark discards — including the dead.
scour-slug.spawnMessage: {Name} hauls itself into view, leaving a clean, wet track over the stone.

messages: hauls itself over the stone, leaving a clean track
gropes blindly at a smear of lichen
recoils from the light, then creeps on

glut-slug.name: a glut-slug
glut-slug.description: A scour-slug gorged past all reason — bloated to the size of a hog on the endless dead of the midden, its grey hide stretched thin and weeping a clear, stinging slime that fumes where it drips and etches pale scars into the stone. The dark the warding never reached has worked into it: it no longer waits for the dead but humps itself toward anything warm and living, and the slime it leaves is no longer clean but sour, eating at whatever it touches.
glut-slug.spawnMessage: {Name} humps its bloated bulk into view, weeping caustic slime as it comes.

messages: humps itself blindly toward your warmth, slime fuming
drags its bulk over the stone, etching a sour, smoking track
swells and contracts, weeping caustic slime from every seam

great-glut.name: the Great Glut
great-glut.description: The thing at the bottom of the midden was a slug once. Now it is a hill of grey, glistening flesh that fills its pit wall to wall, swollen over ages on everything the deep gave up to it, its hide split and re-healed in glassy ridges and running everywhere with the same sour, smoking slime its lesser kin weep — but here in a flood, pooling and hissing on the stone. It has no eyes and needs none; it knows the warmth of you, and the whole vast bulk of it turns, slow and certain, and begins to come. Whatever the deep-folk meant this pit to clean, it long ago stopped cleaning and started keeping.

messages: heaves its bulk forward, a tide of smoking slime fanning out before it
splits along an old ridge and reseals, weeping a fresh flood of caustic ooze
presses its blind, dripping front toward your warmth

cave-centipede.name: a cave centipede
cave-centipede.description: A whip-fast length of armoured segments and skittering legs, fond of the dim edges where prey strays from the light. Its bite festers.
cave-centipede.spawnMessage: {Name} pours in over the rock on a hundred skittering legs.

messages: pours over the rock on a hundred legs
rears, antennae testing the air
skitters along the dim edge of the light

centipede-broodmother.name: a centipede broodmother
centipede-broodmother.description: A centipede grown vast and slow with laying — a body the length of a forearm and thick as a wrist, its pale armoured segments swollen and dark in the seams. She does not range as her young do; she holds the nest, and the nest holds her, and from the runs in the wall she calls up more of her brood than any one delver should care to meet. Her bite carries the same festering venom, slowed and concentrated by her bulk.

actions.verb: shudders along her length, and the wall-runs spill a skittering of young

messages: coils protectively over the brood-litter
rears, every antenna testing the air at once
drags her swollen length across the husks

pale-salamander.name: a pale salamander
pale-salamander.description: A blind, milk-white salamander haunting the damp near the pools, its skin flinching from any glow. Bring a light and it is gone into a crack before you place it.
pale-salamander.spawnMessage: {Name} creeps out of the damp, its blind head flicking side to side.

actions.verb: flinches from the light and slips into a crack

messages: basks still in the damp
flicks a pale, blind head
slides closer to the water

tremor-mole.name: a tremor-mole
tremor-mole.description: A pallid, eyeless burrower that reads the stone by footfall. It surfaces only in the dark; the faintest light sends it digging.
tremor-mole.spawnMessage: The grit shifts and crumbles, and {name} noses up out of the stone.

actions.verb: startles and digs out of sight

messages: reads the stone with trembling whiskers
noses blindly through the grit
freezes, listening through the rock

pale-crayfish.name: a pale crayfish
pale-crayfish.description: A translucent, eyeless crayfish picking through the silt of the deep pools, claws raised at anything that nears its water.
pale-crayfish.spawnMessage: {Name} scuttles up out of the black water, claws raised.

messages: clacks its pincers
scuttles sideways over the silt
raises its claws in threat

blind-cave-fish.name: a blind cave-fish
blind-cave-fish.description: A pale, eyeless fish drifting the still pools, reading the water for whatever falls in. A staple catch for camps that learn the trick of it.
blind-cave-fish.spawnMessage: {Name} drifts up out of the black water.

skittish.verb: slips away into the black water

messages: drifts in a slow, blind circle
noses at the surface
hangs still in the black water
flicks away into the black water
shies from the ripples

actions.verb: drifts off down the current

old-grinder.name: the Old Grinder
old-grinder.description: A boulder-sized stonebug so ancient it has worn a smooth hollow in the stone where it feeds, the rock polished mirror-flat by years of slow circuits. Its plates are layered with old growth — lichen, mineral stain, the calcified scars of a hundred encounters — and decades of grinding ore-rich rock to graze have lodged a knot of swallowed glimmer deep in its gut, turned slow to crystal. It does not hurry. It does not flee. It simply persists, and anything foolish enough to strike it discovers very quickly that persistence has teeth.

messages: grazes along the worn hollow, mandibles working in a slow, ancient rhythm
trundles a deliberate half-circle, plates grinding over smooth stone
pauses, tucks its legs, and sits completely still — indistinguishable from a boulder

actions.verb: grinds slowly forward

elder-stonebug.name: an elder stonebug
elder-stonebug.description: A stonebug grown old and huge in the warded quiet, its overlapping grey plate darkened and mineral-stained along the seams where decades of grinding ore-rich rock have worked into the shell. It grazes the beds at the head of its kind and keeps a slow watch over them — and when one of the lesser bugs is set upon, it does not curl away but turns, plates grinding, and puts its bulk between the threat and its brood.
elder-stonebug.spawnMessage: {Name} grinds its way into view, plates scraping over the stone.

messages: grazes the beds with slow, grinding mandibles
trundles a deliberate half-circle, plates scraping stone
settles between the lesser bugs and goes still as a boulder

actions.verb: grinds slowly off through the beds

pallid-hunter.name: a pallid hunter
pallid-hunter.description: A lean, eyeless predator built for the total dark — long-limbed and nearly silent, navigating by scent and the faint vibration of footsteps through stone. Its bleached, bone-white hide is broken along the spine and flanks by dull nodes of glimmer grown into the flesh, lightless and cold until they are cut from it — crystal pushing up through old snow. Its kind belong deeper than this; the deep has been working on this one a long time, and that it climbed so high is its own quiet warning. Any light at all stops it in its tracks, and a bright source drives it into a frenzy of flinching and retreat. In the dark it is another matter entirely.
pallid-hunter.spawnMessage: The dark shifts, and {name} pads in on silent feet.

actions.verb: pads off into the dark

messages: lifts its head, nostrils working at the air
tilts its skull toward you, listening with its whole body
flinches from the edge of the light, then stills

umbral-trader.name: Mallki the qhatuq
umbral-trader.description: Tall and spare, grey-skinned and unhurried, with the wide, lightless eyes of a thing born well below the reach of any sun. A faint blue-green light moves under his skin where the dark presses closest — not glimmer's work, only the deep's own glow. He is the first of the Umbrals a delver meets on the way down: a qhatuq, a trader, who keeps a lamp-lit hollow by the river and a tended garden behind it. He watches the surface-folk with neither warmth nor malice — only the patience of someone who was here long before the Rush, and expects to be here long after.

messages: traces a slow glyph in the air, the light beneath his skin pulsing once as he does
watches you with wide, lightless eyes and says nothing
turns a glimmer-lamp down a notch, easing the glare
sorts a handful of pale river-shells with long, careful fingers
says, slow and careful: "Yaku-runa." — water-folk, the name his people give the delvers who follow the river down
turns his glimmer-lamp lower still as the rim-folk turn theirs up
watches the bright-folk scramble for their lamps, unmoved, the light beneath his skin steady
says, slow: "Your people fear the deep breath. Mine were born inside it."
stills, listening to the dark the way one listens to an old song

messages.target: regards your unlit hands without alarm: "You walk as my people do — but your eyes are not ours, yaku-runa. The Tide will blind you. Carry a flame, for your sake, not mine."
messages.room: regards {name}'s unlit hands with calm, lightless eyes.

messages.target: considers your small light: "Enough for a quiet hour. Not for the deep breath that comes. The bright-folk sell stronger; take it."
messages.room: considers {name}'s small light, unhurried.

messages.target: turns his own lamp low as the dark gathers: "Your kin fear this hour. Mine were born in it. Keep your fire close, yaku-runa, and it will pass."
messages.room: turns his glimmer-lamp low as the Tide gathers.

messages.target: goes still as he marks the old blade in your pack, the light beneath his skin dimming low: "That is mine, yaku-runa. I carried it up toward your sun, and the dark took it from my hand on the climb. Bring it here — I would not lose it twice."
messages.room: goes still at the old deep-stone blade in {name}'s pack.

messages.target: turns his face from the brightness of your lantern: "Softly, yaku-runa. Your light presses on my eyes."
messages.room: turns his face from the brightness of {name}'s lantern.

messages.target: tilts his head away from your torch: "Your fire is loud where mine is soft, yaku-runa."
messages.room: tilts his head away from {name}'s torch.

messages.target: regards your wounds without alarm: "The deep takes its toll in blood first, yaku-runa. Pay it less."
messages.room: regards {name}'s wounds with wide, lightless eyes.

messages.target: inclines his head, slow and unhurried: "Trade, or talk. Both are welcome. Both cost time."
messages.room: inclines his head to {name}, slow and unhurried.

messages.target: says, slow and careful: "The river brought you. It will take you back — one way or the other."
messages.room: says something slow and careful to {name} in the rounded Umbral tongue.

umbral-chitin-warden.name: a chitin warden
umbral-chitin-warden.description: A low, broad arthropod — big as one of the old stonebugs Mallki herds — its overlapping plates worked and scored with the same glimmer-glyphs he cuts into stone, marks of craft and not of any deeper change. A thread of woven gut-line runs from a ring set in its carapace to a peg by the forge, long enough to let it walk the hollow. It carries itself between the trader and the dark with the flat, unbothered patience of something that has done this a long time, and bristles its spines at anything that comes at its keeper wrong.

actions.verb: scuttles off over the worked stone

messages: bristles its glyph-scored spines and settles again
drags its gut-line taut, testing the peg
rasps its plates against the worked floor
swivels toward the dark, then loses interest
bristles every glyph-scored spine as the dark presses in
drags its gut-line taut and sets itself toward the deepening black
rasps a low warning-note none of the rim-folk can hear
swivels toward the gathering dark and goes very still

pale-king.name: the Pale King
pale-king.description: A crayfish grown monstrous — longer than a man and armoured like one, a translucent grey-white carapace gone yellow and pitted with age, dragging two claws that could shear a wrist. Years in the cold of the deep lake have crusted its shell with raw glimmer, dull veins of cold mineral light grown into the plate where the water lapped longest, so that it glimmers faintly even in the black. It is what its lesser kin will never live long enough to become: the apex of the still water, blind, ancient, and patient, and it has eaten everything the falls ever offered it.

messages: spreads both claws wide, shell scraping on the bone-littered rock
clacks its pincers in a slow, grinding rhythm
drags itself a half-circle, glimmer-light sliding over its plates
raises a claw the length of a forearm and holds it, waiting

yana.name: Yana, the lost apprentice
yana.description: Tall and grey and wrong, a thing that was an Umbral not so long ago — and not just any: the cut of its face still carries something of Mallki's careful kin, the apprentice who once kept the qhatuq's shelves and learned his glyphs. The deep has had it since. It has stretched gaunt and starved, its wide eyes gone raw and light-shy, its long hands curled to claws; the soft blue-green glow Mallki carries still moves under its skin, the last unbroken thing about it, lighting a face that no longer remembers what it was. It speaks, sometimes — broken scraps of the rounded Umbral tongue, a word for a tool, a word for a name, worn loose of all meaning. It will not know you. It will not know itself. It only knows the dark, and that the light hurts.

messages: shields its eyes and hisses, though there is no light to hurt it
mouths a soft, rounded word in the Umbral tongue, then loses it
claws at the swept-bare shelves as if looking for something
drags a fingertip through the air in the start of a half-remembered glyph, then forgets the rest

elder-gloom-crawler.name: an elder gloom-crawler
elder-gloom-crawler.description: A gloom-crawler grown vast and old in the deep dark, where nothing ever drove it off and the feeding never stopped. Where its lesser kin flow lightly over the stone, this one hauls itself forward in slow, heaving surges, a pallid mass of segmented flesh the length of a man, its eyeless front fringed with feelers that taste the air for warmth and tremor. Age has thickened it past the reach of a torch's sting — it does not flinch from the small light a delver carries, and will not flee it — but a true brightness still burns the pale hide raw. It reads you long before you see it, and it is patient, and it is always hungry.
elder-gloom-crawler.spawnMessage: {Name} hauls itself in out of the dark in slow, heaving surges.

messages: heaves itself forward, flesh dragging over the stone
fans its feelers wide, tasting for warmth
lets out a low, wet rattle that fills the dark
rears its blind front and holds, scenting the air

gloom-touched-crawler.name: a gloom-touched crawler
gloom-touched-crawler.description: One of the warren's pale mass, but wrong. Its flesh has gone the grey of a snuffed coal, and a thread of the deeper dark clings to it the way oil clings to water, never quite settling. This one crawled too long against the thing in the drowned hollow that drinks the light, and the appetite has gotten into it — where its kin only hunger, this one has learned to reach. It gathers the cold dark to its blind front and flings it, and the warmth it tears out of you it seems, for a moment, to feed on. A torch still burns its hide raw, but the dark in it does not care to be seen.
gloom-touched-crawler.spawnMessage: {Name} drags itself into view, a thread of black trailing from its grey flesh.

messages: draws the dark in close about its blind front, and the cold deepens
heaves itself forward, a thread of black trailing from its grey flesh
fans its feelers wide, tasting for warmth
lets out a low, wet rattle, and for a moment your light seems to gutter

the-starving-dark.name: the Starving Dark
the-starving-dark.description: Not a beast but a hole in the light — a coil of living dark that gathered in the dead air beyond the prospectors' camp and learned to feed on the warmth and glow that delvers carry down. It does not bleed and does not breathe; it has no edge the eye can fix on, only a deeper black that shifts against the black, and a cold that leans on the skin like a held breath. Bright light unmakes it, sears it thin and screaming-silent, so it snuffs every flame it can reach and drinks the chamber dry, then closes in the dark of its own making. The crawlers do not serve it. They gather to it the way scavengers trail a killer, drawn to the dark it carries with it. It is the answer to the question the empty camp asks — why the tallies broke off, why no one struck the tents or carried them home. It is the first of its kind a delver meets. It will not be the last.

messages: folds in on itself, and the cold deepens
shifts — a darker dark sliding across the dark
leans toward your light like a thing that is hungry for it
goes utterly still, and the silence presses in

void-shadow.name: a void shadow
void-shadow.description: Lesser kin of the Starving Dark, and never the only one for long — a shred of living black that peels itself out of the unlit air when the Tide drowns the deep and a delver's own light has failed. The eye slides off it; it is a deeper dark sliding against the dark, ringed in a cold that leans on the skin. It cannot bear a flame: bright light sears it thin and soundless and drives it off, so it snuffs what it can reach and closes in the black of its own making. Bold in the dark, and it follows. When the Tide recedes, it sinks back into the air it came from.
void-shadow.spawnMessage: The dark thickens and folds — {name} peels itself out of the unlit air beside you.

messages: folds in on itself, and the cold deepens
shifts — a darker dark sliding across the dark
leans toward your light, hungry for it
goes utterly still, and the silence presses in

void-leech.name: a void leech
void-leech.description: The least of the Starving Dark's get, and never one where there could be ten — a mote of hungry black no bigger than a clenched fist, wept out of the drowned air only where the Tide has pooled at its very deepest. It does not sear or savage as its shadow-kin do; it drifts close and settles, and drinks — drawing the warmth of will straight out of a delver, the spark a caster burns to work by. Frail as smoke, a single blow bursts it, but the deep dark breeds them faster than a hand can strike a light, and a cloud of them can leave a mage hollow and wrung dry in the black. When the Tide recedes they thin back into the air that bore them.
void-leech.spawnMessage: A mote of hungry dark peels loose from the black and drifts against you — {name}.

messages: clings closer, and a cold, drinking pull tugs at the marrow of your will
pulses once, dim and glutted
drifts against your skin, patient and hungry
shivers — a fleck of dark drinking the dark

pale-mother.name: the Pale Mother
pale-mother.description: The mother of the warren, grown monstrous in the wet dark where nothing ever drove her off and the feeding never stopped — a pallid, glistening bulk the size of a man curled on itself, her segments swollen and straining, the seams of her gone dark with the deeper black that has soaked up out of the drowned stone into all her kind. She does not range as her brood do; she holds the sump, and the sump holds her, and from the egg-strings strung along the walls she calls up more of her young than any one delver should care to meet. Her bite carries the same rot her children's do, slowed and thickened by her bulk into something that festers. She is blind, and old, and she has never once gone hungry.

actions.verb: heaves along her swollen length, and the egg-strings split to spill a fresh skittering of brood

messages: coils protectively over the heaped brood-litter
heaves her swollen length across the husks, egg-strings trembling
fans her feelers wide, tasting the dark for warmth
lets out a low, wet rattle that the whole sump seems to answer

nawpa.name: Ñawpa, the Hollow Watch
nawpa.description: Taller and greyer and further gone than the lost apprentice in her dead hollow far above — a deep-dweller that was an Umbral glimmer-singer once, and has kept this threshold so long the deep has all but finished with it. Its frame is stretched gaunt and wrong, its wide eyes gone raw and shy of a light it no longer needs to see, and the soft blue-green glow of the deep-folk still moves under its skin, the last unbroken thing about it. It does not conjure light the way the trader's kin do, nor drink it like the things of the warren; it builds. It sets worked plates upon the stone and drags the cold glimmer up through them until the dead shells stand and shield it, and it strikes with spears of frozen light dragged raw from the shards. It mouths broken scraps of the rounded Umbral tongue over its work — a word for a glyph, a word for a name — worn loose of all meaning. It will not pass the threshold it keeps; it will not let you pass it either.

actions.verb: sets a worked plate on the stone and drags the cold glimmer up through it, until the dead shell shudders and stands

messages: shields its wide eyes and hisses, though no light reaches this deep
mouths a soft, rounded word of warding in the Umbral tongue, then loses the thread
drags a fingertip through the air in the start of a glyph, and the dark seems to thicken
sets a clawed hand on a worked plate and goes very still, listening

grave-husk.name: a grave-husk
grave-husk.description: An Umbral that the dark finished with and stood back up. The self is long gone — this is only the emptied body the Hollowing left behind, grey skin drawn tight over a frame gone gaunt and crooked, the soft deep-glow under it snuffed to a cold, dead nothing. It shuffles out of the graves in slow, lurching steps, wide raw eyes fixed on nothing and everything, and comes for the one warm thing in the dark. It does not hunger the way a beast does. It only reaches, the way the dark that made it reaches, and what little light you carry stings the dead flesh raw.
grave-husk.spawnMessage: {Name} lurches up out of the graves, dead eyes fixed on nothing and everything.

messages: lurches a step nearer, dead eyes fixed on your warmth
reaches with grey, crook-fingered hands and closes on nothing
works its slack jaw around a word it no longer remembers
shudders, and the cold pours off it

bound-husk.name: a bound husk
bound-husk.description: One of the old dead, drawn up whole from the deep niches where the deep-folk once laid their honoured ones to keep — bound tight in wrappings gone black and stiff, and cased where the wrappings failed in a rind of the cold dark itself, layered on like a second hide over ages of lying in it. The Hollowing worked slower on these, and left more standing: the frame is broad and heavy still, and the dark that fills it now has crusted the old flesh into something a blade turns on. It comes on without hurry and without stopping, and where its lesser kin reach with dead hands this one strikes to break.
bound-husk.spawnMessage: {Name} drags itself up from a deep niche, black wrappings trailing.

messages: drags itself forward, black wrappings trailing from a heavy frame
raises a wrapped arm cased in cold dark and brings it down
grinds a slow half-turn, the rind of dark cracking and resealing over the old flesh
stands utterly still a moment, as if remembering it was once kept

crypt-lurker.name: a crypt-lurker
crypt-lurker.description: Not one of the standing dead but the dark that keeps them company — a long, folded thing of shadow and dry sinew wedged back in a grave-niche or pressed flat to the ceiling of a tomb, so still and so lightless that the eye passes over it as more empty dark. It waits where the dead are thickest, reading the black by tremor and the warmth of blood, and it will hang there unmoving until a delver's light strays close enough. Then it comes down without a sound. Bright light finds it out and sears the shadow-sinew raw, so it clings to the deepest dark it can find — and in the necropolis, the dark is deep.
crypt-lurker.spawnMessage: Back in a grave-niche, {name} unfolds a length of shadow-sinew, then stills.

messages: shifts its grip in the niche, a deeper dark against the dark
unfolds a length of shadow-sinew, then stills
draws the cold in close about itself and waits
watches from the black, unblinking and unlit

aya-keeper.name: the Aya-Keeper
aya-keeper.description: It kept this place once, and in a broken, ruined way it keeps it still — a deep-folk glimmer-singer the Hollowing has taken most of the way down, further gone than the lost apprentice above but not yet the whole way to shadow. It stands taller than a man and wrong, grey and gaunt, the soft deep-glow guttering under a skin gone slack, and it moves among the graves with the ghost of an old tending in its hands. Where a delver sees only the standing dead, the Aya-Keeper sees its charges: it sets a clawed hand to a fallen husk and drags the cold dark up through it until the dead thing shudders and stands, and it flings the gathered black at anything that would disturb the ones it keeps. It mouths broken scraps of the rounded Umbral tongue over its work — a word for the dead, a word for a name — worn loose of all meaning. It will not leave the graves. It will not let you profane them either.

actions.verb: sets a clawed hand to a fallen husk and drags the cold dark up through it, until the dead thing shudders and climbs to its feet

messages: shields its guttering eyes and hisses, though your light is faint
mouths a soft, rounded word over the graves in the Umbral tongue, then loses the thread
passes a clawed hand across a wrapped husk, as if setting it right
draws the cold dark of the necropolis in close, and the graves seem to lean toward it

interred-dark.name: Supay, the Interred Night
interred-dark.description: Whatever was laid in the great crypt at the top of the necropolis is not laid there anymore. The dark of this place did not merely take an Umbral, as it took the husks below — it gathered in the sealed tomb over an age of lightless keeping, drank down the one interred there, and stood up wearing the shape of the deep's oldest fear. It is kin to the Starving Dark of the shallow warren, but that thing was a hole in the light newly opened; this is the same appetite grown vast and patient in a sealed grave, wound through with the frozen glimmer of the one it unmade. The eye cannot hold it — a coil of living black that leans a killing cold on the skin, and where it moves the crypt goes darker still. Bright light unmakes it, sears it thin and screaming-silent, so it drinks the chamber dry and closes in a dark of its own making. The deep-folk sealed this crypt and warded the way to it, and lit the ward-post at the bridge's end, and it was never enough. It is the thing the wardings were set against. It has been waiting a very long time to be let out.

actions.verb: peels a shred of itself loose, and a lesser dark unfolds from the black to hunt beside it

messages: folds in on itself, and the killing cold of the crypt deepens
shifts — a darker dark sliding across the dark, threaded through with cold frozen light
leans toward your light like a thing that has been starved of it for an age
goes utterly still, and the whole crypt holds its breath

camp-miner.name: Bricke the pickman
camp-miner.description: A broad, stooped old prospector with a pick worn thin at the head and hands gone to horn from a lifetime of swinging it. He came down with one of the first waves and, unlike most, is still swinging — the deepest working pick in the Abyss, and proud of it. Lately he spends near as much time watching the deep-folk across the water as digging: their stonework shames his own, and he has been trying, slow and stubborn, to learn the way they read a seam. He'll trade a delver the tools and the light to cut their own, and grumble the whole time about what the young ones don't know.

messages: works the seam with a slow, steady swing
spits on his palms and takes up the pick again
squints across the water at the deep-folk's stonework, frowning
traces a vein in the rock with one horny finger, muttering
tests the edge of his pick against a calloused thumb
turns his lamp-pole up and sets his back to the rock as the dark comes on
stops swinging and watches the black past the stakes, pick held low
mutters that a working man keeps his light close when the deep breathes

messages.target: jabs his pick toward your dark hands: "No light, this deep, with the Tide coming? That's a fool's way to end up a story. Buy a lamp off me first."
messages.room: jabs his pick toward {name}'s unlit hands.

messages.target: nods at the hatchet on your belt: "Good steel. Keep it sharp — the deep rock eats a soft edge."
messages.room: nods approvingly at the hatchet on {name}'s belt.

messages.target: eyes your wounds: "You've been below, then. Get Wren at the rope-head to look at that before it goes bad."
messages.room: eyes {name}'s wounds and jerks a thumb east toward the rope-head.

messages.target: leans on his pick: "Deepest working seam in the Abyss, this. Want the tools to cut your own? I sell 'em, and cheaper than dying does."
messages.room: leans on his pick and says something to {name} about the seam.

messages.target: nods across the water: "The deep-folk read this rock like a book I can't. Forty years swinging and I'm still learning off 'em. Don't tell 'em I said so."
messages.room: nods across the water toward the Umbral fields and mutters to {name}.

camp-captain.name: Captain Sella
camp-captain.description: A lean, weathered woman in a patched deep-coat, grey coming into hair she keeps cropped short, with the still watchfulness of someone who has counted a great many people down a hole and not all of them back up. She led the expedition that made the whole descent and, when the others wanted to turn for the surface rich, planted a stake here instead and called it Lastlight. It was her doing that the deep-folk across the water let the camp stand — a slow, wordless truce of kept distance and small trades — and she guards it as carefully as she guards the light. Nobody voted her captain. Nobody has argued.

messages: marks a name off a battered roster with a stub of chalk
feeds a fresh coal to the fire-pit and watches it catch
checks the stacked descent-kit with a practised eye
looks east toward the rope-head and the dark past it
weighs a stranger's crystal in her palm without a word
orders every lamp on the ring turned up and counts heads by the fire
stands and watches the dark past the stakes until the Tide passes
tells the camp, quiet and firm, that nobody ropes down till it turns

messages.target: catches your arm: "Tide's in. You don't go over the edge in this, and you don't leave the lamps. Sit by the fire till it turns."
messages.room: catches {name}'s arm and steers them toward the fire.

messages.target: looks you over: "You came back up leaking. Good — up beats the other way. See Wren at the rope-head before you do anything stupid twice."
messages.room: looks {name}'s wounds over and points them east to the rope-head.

messages.target: frowns at your dark hands: "No light and you want to go deeper? Not off my rope-head you don't. Kit up first."
messages.room: frowns at {name}'s unlit hands.

messages.target: measures you with a look: "You made it to Lastlight. Most don't. What you do past the rope-head is your business — but you kit up and mend up here first, or you don't go at all."
messages.room: measures {name} with a long, level look.

messages.target: nods across the water: "We stand here because the deep-folk let us, and they let us because we keep our distance and our word. You'll do the same under my lamps."
messages.room: says a low, firm word to {name} and glances toward the Umbral fields.

camp-alchemist.name: Corvane the alchemist
camp-alchemist.description: A rangy, bright-eyed man with a singed collar, a dozen stains on his sleeves and not one apology for any of them. He is that rare thing — an alchemist who came down the hole himself rather than buy his reagents off those who did, and he'll tell you at length that you cannot know a deep-gathered stuff until you've gathered it in the deep. His bench holds things no Rim alchemist has ever cracked a stopper on, and he grins like a man who means to boil down the whole Abyss one jar at a time. He sells the results, and throws in the theory free whether you want it or not.

messages: swirls a vial against the lamp and watches the colour turn
scribbles a formula on the bench-plank and underlines it twice
sniffs a jar, recoils, and writes something down anyway
grinds a pinch of glimmer-dust and murmurs at how it catches the light
tastes something off the back of his hand, considering
holds a vial of glowing draught up as the dark comes on, delighted at how it shines
trims his bench-lamp higher and keeps right on working through the Tide
mutters that the deep breath does the strangest things to a reagent, and reaches for a fresh jar

messages.target: brightens at your wounds: "Oh, you're a mess — perfect. No no, mostly a jest. Mostly. Here, a regeneration draught, proper stuff, drink it before you fall over."
messages.room: eyes {name}'s wounds with unsettling enthusiasm and reaches for a vial.

messages.target: waggles a glowing vial at you: "In the dark with nothing lit? I bottle light, friend — cheaper than a funeral and far more interesting."
messages.room: waggles a faintly glowing vial toward {name}.

messages.target: leans across the bench, eyes bright: "You cannot know a deep-gathered stuff until you've gathered it in the deep — which I have, so buy from a man who's bled for his reagents."
messages.room: leans across the bench and launches into something at {name}, hands waving.

messages.target: taps a stoppered jar of glimmer-dust: "Down here the light itself is an ingredient. Try getting that to sit still on a Rim bench. Go on — I'll wait."
messages.room: taps a jar of faintly glowing dust and grins at {name}.

camp-healer.name: Wren the mender
camp-healer.description: A quiet, plain-robed woman with steady hands and tired eyes, who set up her post at the very lip of the shaft because that is where she is needed — mending delvers whole before they rope down, and catching what staggers back up. Hers is the surface tradition of magic, the kind paid for in will alone with no glimmer burned: light cantrips, a warding weave, the slow knitting of a wound. She does not delve. She waits at the edge, keeps her light lit, and sends other people's children over it as sound as she can make them. She sells what a delver can carry, and mends what she can for the asking.

messages: traces a slow warding-sign over the mouth of the shaft
rolls fresh bandages by lamplight and stacks them ready
murmurs a mending-cantrip under her breath, testing the weave
watches the black shaft as though listening for someone climbing
warms her hands at the lamp and says nothing
draws a bright warding-light about her post as the dark presses in
keeps her lamp high and her eyes on the shaft through the whole of the Tide
whispers a small light-cantrip and holds it steady against the gloom

messages.target: reaches for you at once: "Hold still — you're hurt worse than you're letting on. Let me knit that before you take it back down."
messages.room: reaches at once for {name}, hands already glowing with a mending-weave.

messages.target: steadies you with a glowing hand: "Sit. Breathe. The shaft will still be there when you're whole."
messages.room: steadies {name} with a softly glowing hand.

messages.target: frowns gently: "No armour, and going over the edge? At least let me lay a warding-weave on you — will alone, no glimmer, it costs you nothing."
messages.room: frowns gently at {name}'s unprotected body.

messages.target: keeps her warding-light high: "Not down the shaft in a Tide, please. Stay in the light. I'll mend you here till it turns."
messages.room: keeps her warding-light high and beckons {name} close.

messages.target: meets your eye, calm and tired: "Nobody ropes down unmended, not off my post. Come here whole or don't go — those are the only two I allow."
messages.room: gives {name} a calm, tired look from the lip of the shaft.

messages.target: says softly: "I don't delve. I wait here, and I mend what comes back. Someone has to be the light at the top of the rope."
messages.room: says something soft to {name} at the edge of the shaft.

the-gloamback.name: the Gloamback
the-gloamback.description: A basking newt grown vast and ancient — the thing the little ones in the shallows will never live long enough to become. It lies half-sunk in the warm water, a slab of pebbled hide the length of a boat, and its broad back is furred over with a living crust of the same luminous moss that hangs from the roof, so that at rest it is nothing but another glowing mound in a hollow full of them. Prey grazes up to the light. Then the mound opens an eye, and a mouth, and the light was never a safe place at all.
the-gloamback.spawnMessage: One of the glowing mounds along the water shifts and opens an eye — {name}, and it has been here the whole time.

messages: sinks lower into the warm water until only its glowing back shows
turns its slow, lamplit gaze on you
gapes a mouth ringed with backward-hooked teeth, then closes it
shrugs the moss on its back into a brighter glow

basking-newt.name: a basking newt
basking-newt.description: A heavy, soft-bodied newt the length of a forearm, its hide mottled warm amber and green — no cave-pale thing but an animal built for warmth and light. It lolls in the shallows and across the warm stone, cropping the luminous moss and soaking up the false dawn, and pays a delver little mind. In the dark it is lost: when the light goes it goes still, and waits for a day the Tide has taken.
basking-newt.spawnMessage: {Name} hauls itself onto a warm rock and settles to bask.

messages: crops a mouthful of glowing moss
spreads its toes against the warm stone
blinks slowly in the false light
slides into the shallows and out again

actions.verb: waddles off to another warm rock

vent-scorpion.name: a vent-scorpion
vent-scorpion.description: A broad, flat scorpion the span of two hands, its shell crusted grey-white with the mineral bloom of the hot vents it shelters in. It runs on scalding stone where nothing else will, claws held low and sting arched high over its back, and comes out for the basking and the wounded alike. The barb weeps a bead of clear venom that never quite dries.
vent-scorpion.spawnMessage: {Name} scuttles out from a crack in the hot rock, sting raised.

messages: arches its sting higher
clicks its claws in warning
sidles across the scalding stone
flicks a bead of venom from the barb

glow-midge.name: a cloud of glow-midges
glow-midge.description: Not one thing but a thousand — a hanging, turning cloud of pale midges no bigger than a grain each, drawn up out of the gulf-dark to any glow they can find and to the warmth of anything that bleeds. Alone they are nothing; in their thousands they whine in the ears, crawl at the eyes, and open a hundred small stinging bites at once, and they do not disperse for long.
glow-midge.spawnMessage: A cloud of pale midges gathers out of the bright air, whining, and turns toward the warmth of you.

messages: draws into a denser, whining knot
settles on every patch of bare skin at once
drifts toward the brightest of the moss-glow
boils up thicker for a moment, then thins

steam-mantis.name: a steam-mantis
steam-mantis.description: A hunting insect grown to the size of a hound, pale green and glass-smooth, so still against the glowing moss that it passes for a stalk of it until it moves. It stands in the lure-light where the midges are thickest and lets the gulf bring its food to it — and takes whatever larger thing comes to the food. Its forelimbs fold away like sheathed sickles, edged inside with rows of black hooks; when they open, they open faster than the eye.
steam-mantis.spawnMessage: A pale stalk of moss you never questioned unfolds all at once — {name}, forelimbs opening like a pair of hooked blades.

messages: sways slowly from side to side, tracking you
cleans a hooked forelimb with unhurried care
folds its blades tight against the pale glow of its body
clicks, and a drift of half-eaten midges falls from its jaws

ember-snapper.name: an ember snapper
ember-snapper.description: What the fruiting canopy comes to, given years and meat enough: an old emberfruit bloom grown fat and rooted and no longer content to wait on the light for its living. Its petals fold back off a wet red throat rimmed with a fringe of inward-curving thorns, and among them it still bears the canopy's fruit — dim-glowing globes gone overripe and heavy, sugared on whatever the plant last caught and dissolved. It cannot walk and does not try to; it sits where it seeded, warm and patient, and waits for something living to lean close to the sweetness. Then it shuts.
ember-snapper.spawnMessage: One of the fruit-heavy blooms among the fronds turns its thorny mouth toward you — {name}, and it was never only a flower.

messages: leans, slow as a sunflower, toward the warmth of you
drips a bead of clear sap from a thorn-tip
shudders once, petals flexing, and stills
breathes out a sweetish, rotten reek

gorgon.name: The Gorgon
gorgon.description: The oldest bloom in the Gloaming, and the last thing the fruiting canopy ever comes to. Where the ember-snappers are single greedy mouths, this is a whole rooted body — a knot of woody trunk the girth of a man, sunk into a rich seam of raw glimmer and grown huge on the cold light and the heat together. From it rises a crown of vines thick as a wrist and longer than a room, and they do not hang: they lift and sway and quest through the warm air on their own account, each tipped with a fanged, fruit-lit head that turns to follow the warmth of you, so the whole plant reads as a nest of pale snakes weaving off a single dreaming stem. It cannot leave the seam it feeds on. It does not have to — it simply reaches, and where its reach falls short it lets a length of itself go, to come and find you on its own.
gorgon.spawnMessage: The great stem in the glimmer-light shudders, and its whole crown of vines rears up off the stone at once — a dozen fanged heads swinging round toward you, {name}, and every snake of it is hungry.

actions.verb: heaves, and its coiled vines peel up off the stone one after another — snake-headed and questing, each cutting itself loose to come hunting on its own

messages: turns a dozen fanged heads slowly toward the warmth of you
drips clear sap from a hundred thorns, and the whole crown sways
settles its roots deeper into the glimmer-seam and drinks
breathes out a wave of sweetish, carrion-rank heat

gorgon-tendril.name: a lashing tendril
gorgon-tendril.description: A length of the great bloom's crown cut loose to hunt — a wrist-thick vine as long as a man, moving upright and head-first like a snake that has forgotten it was ever a plant. Its tip is a small fanged mouth ringed with inward-curving thorns and still lit by one overripe fruit, and it drags the wet, trailing stump of its own severed root behind it as it comes. It cannot last long parted from the stem. It does not seem to care.
gorgon-tendril.spawnMessage: A vine drops off the crown, rights itself head-high, and comes weaving for you — {name}.

messages: rears back, fanged head weaving
snaps at the air between you
drags its severed root a length closer with a wet sound

ember-mote.name: an ember mote
ember-mote.description: A drifting knot of living heat, no bigger than a fist — a core of white-orange fire trailing sparks that gutter and relight. It hangs over the molten lake as if it belonged to it, turning slowly to face whatever comes near.
ember-mote.spawnMessage: A spark lifts off the lava, gathers itself, and becomes {name}.

actions.verb: drifts off across the pool

messages: sheds a spray of sparks
pulses brighter, then dims
circles the pool's edge

halloran.name: Old Halloran
halloran.description: A gaunt old prospector gone half to root down here, brown and knotted as the stalks he tends, with a white beard stained emberfruit-gold at the corners and old eyes gone pale and rheumy in the underlight. He came down early — before the main Rush, when only a handful chanced the hole — to dig, and found a garden instead, and stayed to learn it: his pockets rattle with seed-pods and dried specimens, a bundle of field-notes hangs at his hip on a thong, and he keeps up a low, constant mutter, more to his stonebugs and his fronds than to the rare prospector who wanders in this deep.

messages: pinches a dead frond off at the stem and tucks it into a pouch
murmurs something to the nearest stonebug and pats its plated back
holds an emberfruit up to the light, turning it, and frowns at what he sees
licks a pencil-stub and adds a line to the notes hung at his hip
presses a thumb to the soil of a bed and tastes it, considering
sorts a handful of seed-pods by some order only he knows
talks the fronds down low and easy, the way a man gentles a nervous animal
goes round the croft turning the lamp-wheels up, muttering that the green can't be trusted to hold
cocks his head at the roof as the canopy-glow starts to gutter, and reaches for the nearest lamp
hushes the pens as the light thins, and the stonebugs pull their plates tight
sets his back to the lit hearth and watches the dark come on with the patience of long habit

messages.target: eyes the great fang jutting from your pack and goes very still: "That's it, then — that's her tooth, no mistaking the glimmer worked into it. Bring it here, let me see... aye. The runs'll go quiet a while, and my beds with them. Keep it, friend; I only needed to know it was done."
messages.room: stares at the great fang in {name}'s pack and slowly straightens from the beds.

messages.target: waves your torch back from the beds: "Gently with that flame, friend — the timber's dry as bone and the fruit sulks in fire-light."
messages.room: waves {name}'s torch back from the dry beds.

messages.target: clicks his tongue: "No light on you, this deep? That's how the green gets a man. Stand still too long down here and something with teeth mistakes you for fruit."
messages.room: frowns at {name}'s missing light and shakes his head.

messages.target: steers you toward the fire: "Sit, sit — you're bleeding on my beds. Warm water and a roof that's yours a while; there's worse ways to spend an hour."
messages.room: fusses {name} toward the warmth of the hearth.

messages.target: leans close, confiding: "You'll have seen the blooms in the choke — the fat pale ones that don't quite sit like fruit? Same plant as my fronds here, only old, and fed. Give a sweet thing years and meat enough and it learns to bite. Mind which ones you reach for out there."
messages.room: tells {name} something low and earnest about the blooms in the choke.

messages.target: offers a wedge of emberfruit: "Go on. Grew it myself — tame stock, this. It's the wild ones down the slope you want to worry over."
messages.room: presses a wedge of emberfruit on {name}.

messages.target: says, not much minding whether you answer: "Came down to dig, I did — early, before the big Rush, before your crowds ever came. Found this instead, and never saw a reason to climb back up to the noise you lot brought with you."
messages.room: muses aloud to {name} about coming down before the big Rush.

messages.target: takes a worn lead tag down off a nail and presses it into your palm: "You're climbing back up sometime — I'm not. Carry this to Fenn at the reeve's counter, would you? Tell the old scratch-quill Halloran's not lost. Just done with the noise."
messages.room: presses an old lead tag into {name}'s palm with a message for the reeve.
